title: Old Island Pub

content:
Sun  🌞 Farm Oysters will be having its annual oyster benefit for Block Island based non-profits from 2:00 pm to 4:00 pm at Old Island Pub.

 A dozen oysters can be purchased for a donation of $5.

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Sun Farm Oysters will be having its annual Thanksgiving oyster benefit
summary: On Tuesday, November 24, Sun Farm Oysters will be having its annual Thanksgiving oyster benefit for Block Island based non-profits from 2:00 pm to 4pm at Old Island Pub. A dozen oysters can be purchased for a donation of $5.   Please bring a bag to put your oysters in.
content:
On Tuesday, November 24, Sun Farm Oysters will be having its annual Thanksgiving oyster benefit for Block Island based non-profits from 2:00 pm to 4pm at Old Island Pub. A dozen oysters can be purchased for a donation of $5.   Please bring a bag to put your oysters in.

We ask that participating non-profits bring a donation container (with your name one it) to the parking lot at 1:45 pm and retrieve it at 4:30 pm.  A 50$ prize will be given to the non-profit with the most “interesting” donation container.  (Judge’s identity is anonymous.)  Please email with any questions to: cwarfel@aol.com.

author: Robert Lucier
title: 2018 BIC Fair
summary: The BIC Fair has nothing to do with the pen company, but it is worth penciling in.
content:
The BIC Fair has nothing to do with the pen company, but it is worth penciling in.

In this case, BIC stands for Block Island Conservancy, the home-grown conservation organization that helps keep Block Island green, with over 45% of the island currently free from development.  To celebrate, on August 16 from 2-7 pm, BIC is having a fair on the old Solviken property, one of the parcels they’ve returned to its natural state.

It’ll be an old-fashioned good time, with fair games and a cookout sponsored by Poor People’s Pub. There will be a surfing contest at 3 pm (the property abuts Baby Beach, which is Block Island’s best surf location), and will be both live and silent auctions (live auction begins at 5 pm).

Entry is free. Fair games are $2 per ticket.

meta.description: Block Island Conservancy (BIC) Fair on August 16, 2018.

address: Corn Neck Rd New Shoreham New Shoreham

author: Austin Crawford
title: Handel's Messiah at Block Island School
summary: Join us for an unforgettable musical experience as RICCO presents Handel's Messiah in its entirety. This timeless masterpiece, filled with powerful choruses and moving solos, has been a beloved tradition for centuries. Whether you’re familiar with the "Hallelujah" chorus or are discovering this magnificent work for the first time, this performance promises to inspire and uplift. Performed by our talented vocalists and accompanied by a professional orchestra, this concert is not to be missed.
content:
Join us for an unforgettable musical experience as RICCO presents Handel's Messiah in its entirety. This timeless masterpiece, filled with powerful choruses and moving solos, has been a beloved tradition for centuries. Whether you’re familiar with the "Hallelujah" chorus or are discovering this magnificent work for the first time, this performance promises to inspire and uplift. Performed by our talented vocalists and accompanied by a professional orchestra, this concert is not to be missed.

 Purchase your tickets today and celebrate the joy and spirit of the season with us!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Harp Concert performed by Lily Neill at Island Free Library
summary: LILY NEILL has captivated audiences around the world with the dynamism and breadth of her performances, and her infectious passion for the harp. She has played for world leaders and ambassadors, luminaries in the world of music, young music students, and listeners who never dreamed they would ever hear a harp.
content:
LILY NEILL has captivated audiences around the world with the dynamism and breadth of her performances, and her infectious passion for the harp. She has played for world leaders and ambassadors, luminaries in the world of music, young music students, and listeners who never dreamed they would ever hear a harp.

 

 FREE EVENT!! Bring the kids, all welcome!

author: Austin Crawford
title: 5th Annual Halloween Block Party at New Harbor Boat Basin/Oar
summary: We are delighted to confirm that our 5th Annual Halloween Block Party will take place as planned. This beloved tradition has grown each year, and we are excited to welcome the community once again for an evening of celebration, connection, and fun. Beyond the fun and festivities, the Block Party has always carried a greater purpose. We are proud to announce that proceeds from this year’s event will launch a new scholarship fund dedicated to supporting students pursuing careers in the trades. It is our hope that this initiative will provide meaningful opportunities and long-term benefits for young people in our community.
content: We are delighted to confirm that our 5th Annual Halloween Block Party will take place as planned. This beloved tradition has grown each year, and we are excited to welcome the community once again for an evening of celebration, connection, and fun. Beyond the fun and festivities, the Block Party has always carried a greater purpose. We are proud to announce that proceeds from this year’s event will launch a new scholarship fund dedicated to supporting students pursuing careers in the trades. It is our hope that this initiative will provide meaningful opportunities and long-term benefits for young people in our community.

author: Austin Crawford
title: 4th of July Parade and Fireworks Fundraiser at Yellow Kittens
summary: Fireworks + Parade Fundraiser @ Yellow Kittens Saturday, May 10th 3-6PM
content:
Fireworks + Parade Fundraiser @ Yellow Kittens Saturday, May 10th 3-6PM

 

Hamburgers, hot dogs, veggie burgers, and snacks will be available for purchase, with proceeds going to make this year's parade and fireworks happen!

 

Don't miss an amazing performance for all ages 3:30-4:30 by Providence Drum Troupe. Musicians and performers will bring some parade energy to your May afternoon!

 

Live raffle drawing with tons of epic prizes!

 

RAFFLE TICKETS are ready for purchase at Yellow Kittens, The Chamber, Traveling Seamstress or by emailing doubleenderbi@gmail.com. Raffle tickets are $5 each or 5 tickets for $20. We have some incredible prizes and more on the way!! No need to be present to win our fabulous raffle prizes so if you can't make next Saturday buy your tickets now! Raffle tickets would make great Mother's Day gifts!

Raffle prizes to date include: Eli’s, The National Hotel, Aldo’s, Kimberly’s, Block Island Oyster Bar, Bodhi Spa, Hammet’s Hotel in Newport, Ellen’s, Odd Fellows Hall, Diamond Blue Surf Shop, Shoals, The Atlantic Inn, Daniel Packer Inn in Mystic, Block Island Ferry, All Favorites Cafe, Jayd Bun, Pasquale’s Ice Cream Place, Martucci Group Restaurants, Yellow Kittens, The Darius Inn, Blue Dory Inn, Mutt Hut, Tin Type Portrait

author: Austin Crawford
title: Oysters & Wine Tasting at Winfield's
summary: Winfield's is hosting a self guided wine tasting on Saturday, November 30th starting at 3pm. The wine tasting is $46 per person for a flight of five wines and the price includes tax & gratuity. Our wine tasting spaces are limited so please use the link below or scan the code on the picture to sign up in advance. Great Salt Pond Oyster Co. will be here serving fresh local oysters that will pair perfectly with the wines. It's going to be fun!
content:
Winfield's is hosting a self guided wine tasting on Saturday, November 30th starting at 3pm. The wine tasting is $46 per person for a flight of five wines and the price includes tax & gratuity. Our wine tasting spaces are limited so please use the link below or scan the code on the picture to sign up in advance. Great Salt Pond Oyster Co. will be here serving fresh local oysters that will pair perfectly with the wines. It's going to be fun!

 

See image below for menu. 

 

Sign up here: 

 

https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfd1q75pWuKgnsOctWSVS6ms9j8_yhoaRsKuHM_LFukg_iy2Q/viewform
